subtitle: Where Granite Hills Became a City of Gods and Kings

panels.shot: vast aerial establishing shot at dawn
panels.action: The city of Hampi spreads across a maze of granite boulders and valleys, the Tungabhadra river gleaming like silver, temple towers and palace platforms rising from the stone like a crown.

dialogue.text: Among the boulder hills of the Tungabhadra, Vijayanagara’s capital Hampi rose—sacred to gods, mighty for kings, and wondrous to travelers from Persia and Portugal.

i18n.hi: तुंगभद्रा की शिलामय पहाड़ियों में विजयनगर की राजधानी हम्पी उठी—देवों की पवित्र भूमि, राजाओं की शक्ति, और फारस से पुर्तगाल तक यात्रियों की विस्मय नगरी।

panels.shot: slow push-in from distant hills toward the river valley
panels.action: Ancient granite hills loom, the Tungabhadra curls below, scattered shrines cling to rocks as a faint outline of future streets and compounds begins to emerge in the valley.

dialogue.text: Here, stone was old before men dreamed of kingdoms. The Tungabhadra carved a winding path, and boulders stood like silent witnesses, waiting for a city to bind them together.

i18n.hi: यहाँ पत्थर मनुष्यों के राज सपनों से भी पहले बूढ़ा था। तुंगभद्रा ने बलखाती धार बनाई, और शिलाखंड मौन गवाहों की तरह खड़े रहे, शहर के आने की प्रतीक्षा में।

panels.shot: medium wide of Virupaksha temple precinct nestled among boulders
panels.action: The Virupaksha temple tower rises amid rough granite, small shrines and pillared mandapas dot the sacred centre, pilgrims walk between rock and shrine in a landscape shaped as a temple itself.

dialogue.text: Around Virupaksha, the sacred centre grew first. Hills became sanctuaries, paths became processions, and the hard face of granite was softened by worship, story, and carved stone.

i18n.hi: वीरुपाक्ष के चारों ओर पहले पवित्र केंद्र फला। पहाड़ियाँ तीर्थ बनीं, पगडंडियाँ जुलूस बनीं, और कठोर ग्रेनाइट भक्ति, कथा और मूर्तिकला से नरम पड़ गई।

panels.shot: bird’s-eye over the broader Hampi core showing distinct zones
panels.action: From above, a broad sweep of land reveals temple districts, walled royal compounds, water channels and terraced fields forming a vast, interconnected tapestry across roughly thirty square kilometres.

dialogue.text: By the height of Vijayanagara, Hampi stretched across valleys and ridges. Sacred centre, royal enclosures, and fields were woven together, each zone feeding the others with grain, ritual and power.

i18n.hi: विजयनगर के चरम पर हम्पी घाटियों और पहाड़ियों पर फैल चुका था। पवित्र केंद्र, राज प्रांगण और खेत एक-दूसरे में बुने थे, हर भाग दूसरे को अन्न, अनुष्ठान और सत्ता से पोसता था।

panels.shot: low-angle wide inside the Royal Centre with platforms and walls
panels.action: Massive stone walls frame the Royal Centre, broad raised platforms and pillared audience halls stand empty but imposing, suggesting processions, courts and festival gatherings.

dialogue.text: South of the temples lay the Royal Centre. Here kings stood above their hosts, on high platforms and pillared halls, turning the rocky ground into a theatre of rule and ceremony.

i18n.hi: मंदिरों के दक्षिण में था राज केंद्र। यहाँ ऊँचे मंचों और खंभेदार मंडपों पर राजा अपने सेनापति-दल से ऊपर खड़े, पत्थरीली ज़मीन को राज और समारोह के रंगमंच में बदल देते थे।

panels.shot: sweeping hero wide of the Mahanavami Dibba platform
panels.action: The colossal stepped mass of the Mahanavami Dibba rises under a clear sky, decorated with carved processions, while open space around it hints at crowds once gathered below.

dialogue.text: On the Mahanavami Dibba, the city’s power was displayed. Festival processions climbed its steps, armies and dancers moved below, and the king watched over an ordered world laid out in stone.

i18n.hi: महानवमी डिब्बा पर शहर की शक्ति नजर आती थी। उत्सव जुलूस उसकी सीढ़ियाँ चढ़ते, नीचे सेनाएँ और नर्तक चलते, और राजा पत्थर में बिछी सजी-संवरी दुनिया पर दृष्टि जमाए रहता।

panels.shot: medium wide of elephant stables and nearby structures
panels.action: A long row of domed elephant stables stands in line, their arches opening onto a quiet court, with adjacent buildings hinting at the careful order of royal animal quarters.

dialogue.text: Even the royal elephants had their own architecture. Arched stables and nearby quarters showed that in Hampi, war beasts, ritual animals and men were all given measured places.

i18n.hi: राज हाथियों के लिए भी अपनी वास्तु थी। मेहराबदार अस्तबल और पास के कक्ष बताते थे कि हम्पी में युद्ध-हाथी, पूजा-पशु और मनुष्य सबको नापे-तौले ठिकाने मिले थे।

panels.shot: dynamic street-level wide of a temple bazaar street
panels.action: A straight stone-pillared bazaar street stretches from a towering gopuram, lined with mandapas that once housed markets, the geometry of trade anchored to sacred space.

dialogue.text: Bazaar streets ran like stone rivers from temple towers. Commerce flowed beneath pillared corridors, binding the divine centre to the daily needs of grain, cloth and jewels.

i18n.hi: बाज़ार की सड़कें मंदिर-शिखरों से पत्थर की नदियों-सी बहती थीं। खंभेदार गलियारों के नीचे व्यापार चलता, जो देव-केंद्र को रोज़मर्रा के अन्न, कपड़े और रत्नों से जोड़ता था।

panels.shot: wide landscape shot of agricultural fields and irrigation features beyond city
panels.action: Beyond the built core, terraced fields, canals and tanks spread across the valleys, farmers tending crops that stretch towards distant boulder hills.

dialogue.text: Outside the crowded centre, water channels and fields formed a green buffer. Agriculture fed the city, while tanks and canals tamed the river, turning rocky land into a sustaining plain.

i18n.hi: भीड़भाड़ वाले केंद्र से बाहर पानी की नहरें और खेतों की हरियाली घेरा बनाती थी। खेती शहर को खिलाती, और टैंकों व नहरों से नदी को साधकर पत्थरीली भूमि को उपजाऊ समतल में बदला जाता था।

panels.shot: archaeological mapping-style bird’s-eye with subtle overlays
panels.action: A stylized view of Hampi shows countless tiny outlines of shrines, walls, platforms and paths, suggesting the dense network of more than a thousand structures mapped across the terrain.

dialogue.text: Centuries later, scholars traced over sixteen hundred structures in this landscape, revealing how even minor shrines, walls and paths joined to make a vast, planned sacred-royal terrain.

i18n.hi: सदियों बाद विद्वानों ने इस परिदृश्य में सोलह सौ से अधिक संरचनाएँ दर्ज कीं, दिखाते हुए कि छोटे मंदिर, दीवारें और पगडंडियाँ भी मिलकर एक विराट, योजनाबद्ध पवित्र-राज क्षेत्र रचते थे।

panels.shot: evocative mid-shot of distant hills associated with Kishkindha
panels.action: Boulder hills rise, a small shrine sits on a peak, evoking Ramayana-linked Kishkindha stories without any visible battle or figures, just sacred quiet over rough stone.

dialogue.text: Around Hampi, people saw the hills as Kishkindha, realm of the vanaras in Rama’s tale. Sacred geography wrapped the city in story, even as its history followed more earthly paths.

i18n.hi: हम्पी के आसपास लोगों ने पहाड़ियों को रामकथा की वानर-भूमि किष्किंधा माना। पवित्र भूगोल ने शहर को कहानी में लपेटा, जबकि उसका इतिहास धरती से बंधी राहों पर चला।

panels.shot: grand wide of city walls and gateways with distant hills
panels.action: Multiple lines of fortification walls snake along ridges and around valleys, gateways punctuating the stone, suggesting layered defenses enclosing temples, palaces and houses.

dialogue.text: Travelers spoke of many encircling walls guarding the city. Whether seven rings or fewer, Hampi’s fortifications climbed hills and spanned valleys, turning the landscape itself into armor.

i18n.hi: यात्रियों ने शहर को घेरती कई परकोटों की चर्चा की। सात घेरों हों या कम, हम्पी की फौजी दीवारें पहाड़ चढ़तीं, घाटियाँ लांघतीं, और पूरे भू-दृश्य को ही कवच बना देतीं।

panels.shot: medium shot of a Persian envoy entering through a gate (city as main subject)
panels.action: A figure in Persian robes rides through a massive stone gateway into the urban sprawl, dwarfed by fortifications and towers as the city of Hampi rises ahead.

dialogue.text: In 1443, the Persian envoy Abdur Razzaq entered Hampi’s gates. Its walls, gardens and ordered streets astonished him, and he wrote of splendor rare even in the lands of Islam.

i18n.hi: १४४३ में फारसी राजदूत अब्दुर रज़्ज़ाक हम्पी के द्वारों से भीतर आया। इसकी दीवारें, बाग और सजी-संवरी सड़कें उसे चकित कर गईं, और उसने इस वैभव को इस्लामी जगत में भी दुर्लभ लिखा।

panels.shot: slow push-in along lush garden and water features near royal precinct
panels.action: Formal gardens with pools and channels stretch beside royal structures, trees and flowering plants arranged along stone-lined paths, reflecting a deliberate pleasure landscape.

dialogue.text: Razzaq saw gardens laid out with channels and pools, flanking royal courts. Water, shade and fragrance were crafted as carefully as walls, a softness within the austere stone.

i18n.hi: रज़्ज़ाक ने नहरों और तालाबों से सजाए बाग देखे, जो राज दरबारों के किनारे बिछे थे। पानी, छाँह और सुगंध को दीवारों जितनी ही बारीकी से गढ़ा गया था—कठोर पत्थर में नरमी का संसार।

panels.shot: epic wide of city core bursting with markets and temples, with a sea route inset
panels.action: The temple bazaars and royal streets throng with imagined activity, while in the distance ships sail toward the Indian coast, hinting at overseas links that brought foreigners to this inland capital.

dialogue.text: By the early sixteenth century, Hampi drew men from far ports. The Portuguese reached it through coastal kingdoms, stepping from ships to caravan trails that led to this stone-bound capital.

i18n.hi: सोलहवीं सदी की शुरुआत तक हम्पी दूर-दराज़ बंदरगाहों से लोगों को खींचने लगा। पुर्तगाली तटीय राज्यमार्गों से होते हुए आए, जहाज़ों से कारवाँ-पथ पर उतरकर इस पत्थर-बद्ध राजधानी तक पहुँचे।

panels.shot: medium wide of a Portuguese observer in the Royal Centre plaza
panels.action: A Portuguese traveler stands in awe near the Royal Centre, facing monumental platforms and avenues as the ordered expanse of the city stretches beyond.

dialogue.text: Domingo Paes walked these streets around 1520. He compared Hampi to Rome, stunned by its size, palaces and crowds, though such praise was impression, not measured census.

i18n.hi: लगभग १५२० में दोमिंगो पैस इन गलियों में चला। उसने हम्पी की तुलना रोम से की, इसके फैलाव, महलों और भीड़ से अभिभूत होकर—हालाँकि ऐसी प्रशंसा आँकड़ों नहीं, असर की थी।

panels.shot: close-up on cityscape transitions from sacred to royal to rural
panels.action: One continuous panoramic band shows a temple tower, then a royal platform, then irrigated fields, emphasizing how domains flow into one another without sharp separation.

dialogue.text: In Hampi, there were no harsh borders between temple, court and field. Sacred processions passed near granaries; royal roads cut through farming land. The city was a woven whole.

i18n.hi: हम्पी में मंदिर, दरबार और खेत के बीच कठोर सीमाएँ नहीं थीं। पवित्र जुलूस कोठारों के पास से गुजरते, राजमार्ग खेती की ज़मीन चीरते; शहर एक बुना हुआ अखंड था।

panels.shot: reflective dusk aerial of Hampi’s ruins today
panels.action: At dusk, the ruined towers, platforms and broken walls of Hampi stand silent among the boulders, paths still visible, the Tungabhadra shining as the hills cast long shadows.

dialogue.text: Today, Hampi lies in ruins, yet its design remains clear. The stones remember a capital where landscape, faith, rule and agriculture were fused into one vast, living plan.

i18n.hi: आज हम्पी खंडहर है, पर इसकी बनावट अब भी साफ दिखती है। ये पत्थर उस राजधानी को याद रखते हैं जहाँ भू-दृश्य, आस्था, राज और खेती एक विराट जीवंत योजना में घुल-मिल गए थे।

panels.shot: gentle pull-back from a shrine overlooking the wider cityscape
panels.action: A modest shrine on a hill looks down over the spread of ruins and fields, suggesting a city conceived as a sacred landscape as well as a home for thousands.

dialogue.text: Hampi endures as a lesson: a city can be built not just as shelter or fortress, but as a sacred landscape, where daily life, worship and power are all grounded in the very shape of the earth.

i18n.hi: हम्पी एक सबक बनकर टिके है: शहर सिर्फ आसरा या किला नहीं, बल्कि पावन भू-दृश्य भी बन सकता है, जहाँ रोज़मर्रा की ज़िंदगी, उपासना और सत्ता धरती के आकार में ही जड़ें पकड़े हों।

subtitle_i18n.en: Where Granite Hills Became a City of Gods and Kings
subtitle_i18n.hi: जहाँ ग्रेनाइट पहाड़ियाँ देवों और राजाओं का नगर बनीं
